summary: 13 patient encounters across two rooms became 15 archived sessions due to two false-split pairs (Kaden, Toni). Two additional sessions silently combined consecutive patients (Marlene+Tyler, Ann+toe-cyst) without firing multi-patient detection. 5 SOAPs had hallucinated procedure sections; 3 of those cascaded into false billing claims.

soap_procedure_hallucination.root_cause: SOAP-LLM stance filter (v0.10.61) passes COMPLETED candidates whose transcript_quote does not actually evidence procedure performance. Examples: 'You're done, my friend' (farewell) cited for 'Performed physical examination'; 'I can just keep it there now' (unrelated) cited for 'Applied estrogen patch'.
generalizable_fixes: Add explicit prompt rules: physical exam, vital sign measurement, history-taking, and reviewed-imaging are NEVER billable procedures regardless of completion stance.

Strengthen completion_evidence requirement: must contain an action verb in past tense AND a procedure noun within the same sentence (e.g., 'I just injected the cortisone'). Pure aftercare or farewell phrases are insufficient.
Require BOTH doctor_quote AND completion_evidence to mention the procedure noun (anchor on noun, not just stance).
Add the SOAP-LLM's procedure_candidates JSON to replay_bundle.json:soap_result.response_raw_json (currently lost — only formatted output is captured).

soap_to_billing_cascade.root_cause: Hallucinated 'Applied estrogen patch' in SOAP procedure section was read by billing-LLM and classified as im_injection_with_visit, mapping to G372A.
generalizable_fixes: After SOAP-procedure prompt is hardened, this disappears.
Defensive: rule_engine should require a transcript_quote field on procedure features and reject if quote does not contain action+noun match against the procedure type.

billing_llm_stance_blindness.root_cause: Billing-LLM extracts procedures from SOAP S/A/P sections (where future/proposed procedures are discussed) without stance filtering. Marlene: 'vaccination update' Plan-line → G372A; Dorothy: 'Sublocade injection' Plan-line → G373A. Neither was performed today.
generalizable_fixes: Apply the same v0.10.61 stance-filter pattern to billing's clinical_features extraction. Currently only the SOAP prompt has it.
Require LLM to emit procedure[] entries with a transcript_quote AND a stance field, then have rule_engine drop anything not COMPLETED/IN_PROGRESS+evidence.
Cross-validate: a procedure billed must have its noun in the SOAP Procedure section (not just S/A/P). If the SOAP procedure section is empty, no procedure code may be billed.

false_split_conversational_pause.signature: negative gap of 10-11 seconds between consecutive sessions (next session.started_at < prev session.ended_at), small tail (614 / 1692 words), tail starts mid-conversation continuing prev topic.
false_split_conversational_pause.root_cause: Encounter-detection LLM mistakes mid-encounter wrap-up phrases ('we're going to do X', 'let me know if you want', 'we'll switch insulin') for encounter-end signals. These are mid-conversation summaries, not handoffs. The doctor immediately continues the same teaching/discussion.
generalizable_fixes: Tighten detection prompt: encounter-end requires explicit handoff cues (goodbye, see-you-next-time, leaving the room, room-cleanup language) not just topic-summary language.
Tail-similarity gate: before splitting, peek at next 30 seconds of transcript (if buffered) — if topic-embedding similarity to prev tail > 0.85, suppress the split.
Raise small-orphan merge-back threshold from current value to ~2000 words for sessions where prev_session ended <30s before. Negative-gap consecutive sessions are highly likely false-splits.
Add 'consecutive-session-merge' check at session-end: if two sessions for the same physician on the same day overlap or have <30s gap AND combined word count <8000, suggest auto-merge.

signatures: Marlene 0f61f08a: 121-min single-encounter session containing two patients (Marlene + Tyler). Tyler's vaccination content silently absorbed as 'Marlene's son's vaccination'.
Ann ed45d3f6: 52-min session containing Ann + the next patient (toe-cyst drainage). Toe-cyst content silently absorbed as 'Ann's right foot ganglion cyst'.
cross_patient_silent_contamination.root_cause: Multi-patient retrospective check fires ONLY when (a) a merge-back occurred AND (b) merged transcript >= 2500 words. Both Marlene and Ann had merge_back_count=0, so the retrospective check never ran. The standalone multi-patient check at split time also did not fire because no split fired.
generalizable_fixes: Drop the 'after merge-back only' precondition. Run multi-patient retrospective check at any encounter-end where transcript >= 2500 words (or session duration >= 45 min).
On flush-on-stop, ALWAYS run the multi-patient check on the final buffer regardless of merge-back history.
Vision-based DOB invalidation should trigger an immediate split candidate, not just clear the name tracker — a chart switch is strong evidence the room saw two patients.

Sensor-based: room-empty + room-occupied transitions WITHIN a single session window should be logged and cross-checked against detection events. A 30+ min absence followed by speech is almost certainly a new patient.

k_code_dedup_bug.false_pattern: Two K005A entries each with quantity=1 instead of one entry with quantity=2
k_code_dedup_bug.root_cause: rule_engine.rs line 128-154: when features.conditions contains both PrimaryMentalHealth and OpioidWithdrawalManagement (or any duplicate), each maps to ['K005A'] and is pushed separately. There is no dedup pass after the conditions loop.
generalizable_fixes: After all push paths complete, dedupe codes[] by code-string. For per-unit codes (K013/K033/K005/K007), sum quantities. For one-time codes, keep one entry.
Alternative: track which code strings have been added to codes[] in a HashSet during the conditions loop and skip duplicates.

missing_c002a_hospital_followup.root_cause: Dorothy's visit is explicitly post-hospital-discharge follow-up ('two recent hospital visits', 'discharge note not yet received'). C002A exists in ohip_codes.rs:1622 but has no rule mapping in rule_engine.rs, and the LLM extraction prompt in clinical_features.rs has no condition or visit-type for hospital discharge follow-up.
generalizable_fixes: Add ConditionType::HospitalDischargeFollowUp (or VisitType::HospitalDischargeFollowUp).
Map to C002A in rule_engine.
Update clinical_features.rs LLM prompt with examples: 'If patient is being seen for follow-up after a recent hospital admission/discharge, set hospital_discharge_followup=true.'

diagnostic_code_799_fallback.root_cause: diagnostic_tools_model.rs falls back to 799 when LLM-suggested code does not pass cross-validation against primaryDiagnosis text. 3 hits in one day means the cross-validation is too strict OR the primaryDiagnosis text is too sparse to match.
generalizable_fixes: When falling back, pick a body-system code from the SOAP Assessment text (regex: 'pain'+body-part → 729; 'cough'+'shortness'→786; 'depression'→311 etc.) before using 799.
Soft-cross-validation: instead of exact text-match, use embedding similarity threshold to accept the LLM's suggested code when reasonable.
Log every 799 fallback with the SOAP A section so the diagnostic-codes-replay CLI can re-test fixes.

diagnostic_code_786_misgrouping.root_cause: OHIP dx 786 is grouped 'Epistaxis, hemoptysis, cough, dyspnea, masses, shortness of breath' — the SOB description includes 'chest pain' but the diagnostic_codes.rs description does not, AND the prompt at clinical_features.rs:219 lists 786 as 'Respiratory symptoms (cough, dyspnea)' which doesn't match Kaden's MSK rib pain. The LLM incorrectly picked 786 because of 'chest' anatomic match.
generalizable_fixes: Update diagnostic_codes.rs description for 786 to explicitly distinguish 'chest pain (cardiac/respiratory)' from 'chest wall MSK pain (use 729)'.
Update clinical_features.rs prompt: 'Chest WALL pain, rib pain, or chest pain reproducible on palpation → 729. Chest pain associated with respiratory symptoms (cough, dyspnea) → 786.'
Add this pattern as a benchmark fixture in tests/fixtures/benchmarks/

manual_merge_cross_machine_bug.root_cause: local_archive.rs:1495 in merge_sessions() checks `if !session_dir.exists()` against the calling machine's local FS. Cross-machine sessions appear in hybrid history (server-side merge in commands/archive.rs::get_local_sessions_by_date) but are not present on the local FS unless they were created on this machine.
generalizable_fixes: Pre-fetch missing sessions from profile-service via ProfileClient before invoking merge_sessions() in commands/archive.rs::merge_local_sessions.
Alternative: surface a 'this session lives on Room X — switch to that machine to merge' UI error instead of the raw 'Session not found' message.
Better: add a profile-service merge endpoint (POST /sessions/merge) and route cross-machine merges through it.

detection_task_silent_stall.signature: Last detection event at 15:57:32 with complete=True conf=0.95 wc=3027. NO further detection events for 1h 23m, but session stayed active until 17:20:42 with final wc=3786. complete=True did not result in an archived split.
detection_task_silent_stall.root_cause: Unknown — possibly LLM-router transient unreachability that was logged elsewhere, OR detection task crashed mid-split, OR sleep/calendar transition. day_log.jsonl on Room 2 is needed to disambiguate (not synced to profile-service).
generalizable_fixes: Add liveness watchdog on the detector loop: if no detection event in 2x the configured interval, log a heartbeat-failed event and attempt task restart.
Sync day_log.jsonl to profile-service so cross-machine forensic analysis is possible without SSH.
Add a heartbeat counter to performance_summary.json so detection-task-stalls show up in daily aggregates.

observability_replay_bundle_loses_proced.root_cause: replay_bundle.json:soap_result.response_raw stores the FORMATTED SOAP output (S:/O:/A:/P:/Procedure:) instead of the raw LLM JSON. The procedure_candidates array (with stance, walkback, completion_evidence) is parsed and discarded before the bundle is written.
generalizable_fixes: Capture raw LLM JSON output BEFORE format conversion in llm_client.rs::generate_soap_note. Add a new field `response_raw_json` to SoapResult in replay_bundle.rs.

This is critical for forensic analysis of v0.10.61 stance-filter behavior — without it, we cannot tell whether the SOAP-LLM is mis-classifying stance, ignoring the filter rule, or producing ambiguous candidates.

recommended_replay_corpus_uses: labeled_regression_cli --all should fail-on-regression on the 4 false billing claims after each rule_engine change.
encounter_experiment_cli should re-run the 5 procedure-hallucination SOAPs through any new SOAP prompt and assert procedure[] is empty.
detection_replay_cli should replay 48efcdcc + a30cd034 through any new detection prompt and assert complete=False at the split-point words.
multi_patient_replay_cli should replay 0f61f08a + ed45d3f6 through any new retrospective-trigger condition and assert multi-patient split fires.
